beats.text: Text-to-image is conditional generation: given a caption, sample an image from the distribution of images that match it.
The task asks the model to draw a sample x from the conditional distribution p of x given the caption c.
Three families took turns owning it: GANs first, then autoregressive token models, then diffusion after 2021.
GANs gave the first sharp faces but suffered mode collapse and were hard to scale to open-vocabulary prompts.
Autoregressive models predict thousands of image tokens one by one, paying an O of N generation cost.
Diffusion won the open-domain prize: stable to train, mode-covering, and fast once moved into a latent space.

concepts.title: Diffusion for images, in one page

beats.text: A diffusion model is two opposing processes: a fixed forward chain that adds noise, and a learned reverse chain that removes it.
The forward process has a closed form that jumps straight to any timestep, mixing the clean image with Gaussian noise.
The signal fraction alpha-bar slides from about one at the clean image to about zero at pure noise.
The network is trained to predict the noise that was added; the entire loss is a denoising regression.
The same trained denoiser is reused at every timestep; depth in time comes from iteration, not more parameters.
Conditioning injects the caption embedding through cross-attention; the backbone shifted from U-Net to diffusion transformers.

visual.sub: Cross-attention on the text embedding c

concepts.title: Latent diffusion — DALL·E, Imagen, Stable Diffusion

beats.text: Pixel-space diffusion is expensive: a single step on a 512 by 512 color image touches about 786 thousand values.
Latent diffusion stops diffusing pixels: a VAE compresses the image into a small latent, decoded only once at the end.
With downsample factor eight and four latent channels, the value count drops by 48 times before any diffusion runs.
The three landmark systems differ mostly in where they spend compute: CLIP latents, a frozen T5, or a VAE latent.
Classifier-free guidance extrapolates along the conditional direction, walking s times further than the model would alone.
Raising the guidance scale increases prompt adherence but shrinks diversity, and too high it fries textures.
The autoregressive camp treats an image like text: tokenize it with a VQ codebook, then predict the tokens.
A VQ-VAE snaps each latent vector to the nearest codebook entry, turning a 32 by 32 grid into 1024 discrete indices.
A transformer models the token sequence with the same next-token factorization used for language.
The cost is N sequential forward passes; for 1024 tokens that is the full autoregressive tax per image.
Masked models like MaskGIT and Muse unmask many tokens in parallel, collapsing 256 steps into 8 to 12 rounds.
In 2026 pure autoregressive lost the fidelity crown, but is resurgent inside unified multimodal token streams.

visual.sub: Image tokens in the same stream as text

beats.text: Video is an image problem with one extra axis: time, and the whole challenge is temporal coherence.

visual.sub: Sora-class models · denoise the clip jointly

beats.text: Generating frames independently fails: objects flicker, identities swap, and motion is incoherent across frames.
The recipe is latent diffusion lifted into space-time: compress the clip into spacetime patches and denoise them together.
The patch count multiplies temporal chunks by spatial patches, and joint attention costs order N squared.
Doubling clip length quadruples the attention bill, which is why long video is the genuinely hard part.
These systems learn crude physics statistically, but still violate it; latency and cost remain the binding constraints.

visual.label: denoising steps over thousands of patches
